title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built an end-to-end machine learning pipeline to predict single-family home sale prices across California using 100K+ MLS observations, applying geospatial filtering, missingness mechanism analysis, and custom imputation strategies to prepare the data. Engineered polynomial and interaction features, applied geo-clustering to capture spatial pricing patterns, and developed a two-level stacked ensemble combining XGBoost and LightGBM with a Gradient Boosting meta-learner tuned via RandomizedSearchCV and segment-aware weighting. The final model achieved a sub-8% Median Absolute Percent Error, performance comparable to Redfin's published valuation model, and was deployed as an interactive Streamlit web application.

title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built a multi-agent AI system using LangGraph, LangChain, and Docling to ingest and normalize resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and transcripts into a structured PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using an open-source ATS engine powered by sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Engineered prompt workflows across OpenAI and Ollama models to generate explainable gap analyses and compliant resume improvements grounded strictly in existing candidate evidence, preventing AI hallucination. Delivered a terminal UI via Textual for interactive candidate review and documented reproducible ETL pipelines and prompt engineering best practices for reliable AI-assisted hiring analysis.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models (Random Forest and XGBoost) on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, achieving ~90% accuracy with threshold-tuned recall optimization to minimize missed conversions. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, revealing conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent informed consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest predictors of purchase intent, independent of page value metrics, using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: DSC 80 Project Notebook
description: Built a data science pipeline using pandas and scikit-learn to clean, transform, and model a structured dataset, applying techniques such as feature engineering, imputation, and supervised learning to generate predictive insights. Conducted exploratory data analysis with matplotlib and plotly to visualize distributions and relationships, then trained and evaluated machine learning models to assess predictive performance.

title: UCSD Research Lab
description: Conducted research at UC San Diego under Professor Andre Gray, applying statistical modeling and machine learning techniques in Python (Jupyter Notebook) and R to investigate domain-specific research questions. Developed and validated data pipelines, performed exploratory and inferential analyses, and produced reproducible research artifacts to support lab findings. Findings contributed to ongoing academic research initiatives within the lab.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive web map visualizing bike path usage across Cambridge, Massachusetts, using JavaScript with variable-width polylines to encode traffic volume data directly onto the city's cycling infrastructure. The visualization allowed users to identify high-traffic corridors and underutilized bike paths by translating raw traffic counts into proportionally scaled line weights rendered in the browser. This tool provided an accessible, at-a-glance view of cycling patterns to support urban mobility planning and infrastructure assessment.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
